I treated the prior 50 turns as a proposal log, not as proof. The prompt fixes the â€œtame-formatâ€‌ presentation: sigma,tau are distinguished, the tame relation tau^sigma = tau^2 is implicit, later generators must lie in the pro-2 core, and exponents are represented modulo M=85667662080.The prior record leaves one serious unrefuted candidate, while the scalar, plain-د€2, and fixed-د„âˆ’2 variants have recorded finite-quotient failures.

1. Stable algebra: eliminate the recurring reciprocal ambiguity Several prior turns alternated between reciprocal formulas. I think the safest way to write the two disputed calculations is as equations, not informal fractions.

Sign-cover norm coefficient Let V=kerخ؛ be the sign double cover, let t be its deck involution, and restrict to a nontrivial odd tame-inertia character block د„â†¦خ±î€ =1. ForNa (u)=(uد„a)د€,the Fox coefficient isDa=j=0âˆ‘د€âˆ’1(tخ±a)j.

The defining identity is (1âˆ’tخ±a)Da=1âˆ’(tخ±a)د€.Since د€â‰،1(mod2) on t and د€â‰،0(mode) on an e-th odd tame character,(tخ±a)د€=t.

ThereforeDa=(1âˆ’t)(1âˆ’tخ±a)âˆ’1.This is the only direction compatible with the unit/radical argument: 1âˆ’tخ±a is a 2-adic unit because خ±aî€ =1, while 1âˆ’t is radical. Hence Da is radical, so the coefficient contributed byxâˆ’1(xد„âˆ’خ·)د€isâˆ’1+Dâˆ’خ·,a unit. Thus nontrivial odd tame-character blocks still contribute only unit Smith pivots.

Trivial tame-inertia elimination In the د„-invariant block, with T the دƒ-cycle operator and sâ‰،د€(modf), the mod-2 linear relation is(1+Tâˆ’s)X+(1+T)Y=0.

Write f=2a m with m odd. Then sâ‰،1(mod2a),sâ‰،0(modm).

If f is even, s is odd, so 1+Tâˆ£1+Tâˆ’s. The valid elimination is the polynomial Ps (T) determined by (1+T)Ps (T)=1+Tâˆ’s,i.e.Ps(T)=1+T1+Tâˆ’s up to the harmless convention Tâ†”Tâˆ’1. The reciprocal is not legitimate in F2 [T]/(Tfâˆ’1).With h(T)=1+Ps (T)+Ps(Tâˆ’1),for a nontrivial f-th root خ¶,(1+خ¶)h(خ¶)=خ¶âˆ’s+خ¶s+1=خ¶âˆ’s(1+خ¶2s+1).But 2s+1 is odd and 2s+1â‰،1(modm), so gcd(f,2s+1)=1. Hence h has no nontrivial f-th-root zero, and h(1)=1. The trivial block nonsingularity proof is sound with this convention.With these formulas fixed, the prior finite-tame abelianization checks remain consistent:Ue,fabâ‰ƒZ2ef+1âٹ•C2,and, for the sign kernel,(kerخ؛)abâ‰ƒZ22ef+1âٹ•C4.2. New refinement: split the first IA ambiguity into abelian-visible and genuinely nonabelian parts Prior turns correctly isolated the first residual marked IA ambiguity after odd-Hall rigidity. I think the useful refinement is to separate what is already visible in equivariant abelianization modulo 4 from what remains genuinely nonabelian.Letخ”e,f=âں¨دƒث‰,د„ث‰âˆ£د„ث‰e=1,دƒث‰f=1,دƒث‰د„ث‰دƒث‰âˆ’1=د„ث‰2âں©,eآ odd,eâˆ£2fâˆ’1.

Write f=2a m with m odd, let

H=âں¨د„ث‰,دƒث‰2aâں©be the odd Hall subgroup, and P=خ”e,f/Hâ‰ƒC2a.

In the reduced first graded piece,L1â‰ƒF2[خ”e,f]âٹ•F2 bâٹ•F2S,with marked S=دƒf, the quadratic initial Demuإ،kin form isq=gâˆˆخ”e,fâˆ‘Xg[2]+[b,S].

The first marked IA ambiguity reduces toAe,fâ‰ƒH1(P,L2H),L2=(L1[2]âٹ•âˆ§2L1)/âں¨qâں©.

For f even, the nonfree part of L2His(L2H)nonfreeâ‰ƒJ1âٹ•2âٹ•J2 aâˆ’1âٹ•I(e,f),I(e,f)=gcd(e,2f/2+1),where Jr is the unipotent indecomposable F2[C2a]-module of length r.

The two J1 -summands are the universal square directionsb[2],S[2].

These are visible in Uab/4: changing the action by b[2]or S[2]changes the induced mod-4 abelian action by 2b or 2S. Thus these two directions should be fixed by an equivariant local-class-field-theory comparison on Lأ—/(Lأ—)4. They are not the deepest part of the formation gap.The genuinely nonabelian first-stage directions are indexed by involutionsخ´j=د„ث‰jدƒث‰f/2,j(1+2f/2)â‰،0(mode).

For each such خ´j, defineZj=hâˆˆHâˆ‘[Xh,Xhخ´j].

These Zj are commutator classes, so they vanish in abelianization and are invisible to Uab/4, the sign-kernel Smith form, and cyclotomic torsion checks. They are exactly the first genuinely nonabelian mixed-tame obstruction directions.This sharpens Turn 50â€™s proposed next test. In (e,f)=(5,4), the first IA ambiguity has seven dimensions, but two are abelian-square directions. After the equivariant Uab/4 action is fixed, the hard comparison is the five involution-indexed commutator coordinates attached toد„ث‰jدƒث‰2,jâˆˆZ/5.

Changing the tame Frobenius lift byدƒث‰â†¦د„ث‰kدƒث‰ translates the labels by jâ†¦j+k(2f/2âˆ’1)(mode),so these five coordinates must be compared only after choosing the same Frobenius-lift gauge on the arithmetic side.

3. Current status The dynamic candidate remains the only serious unrefuted word. It has passed the recorded rank, cup-product, abelianization, sign-kernel, and cyclotomic-orientation checks, once the two quotient formulas above are written in the correct direction. But none of those checks proves the final profinite presentation. The remaining issue is a dyadic formation/rigidity theorem, or an explicit arithmetic comparison, identifying the candidateâ€™s exact filtered (دƒ,د„)-operator action with the arithmetic Frobenius/tame-inertia action over all finite tame subextensions.The next discriminating arithmetic test should not be a giant matrix comparison. For (e,f)=(5,4), it should first fix a concrete tame degree-20 extension and a Frobenius lift, compare the two abelian-square coordinates through Lأ—/(Lأ—)4, and then compare the five nonabelian Zj -coordinates through GL (2)/GL(2)3 or GL (2)/GL(2)5.

ESTABLISHED: The live dynamic candidate remains unrefuted. The stable formulas areDa=(1âˆ’t)(1âˆ’tخ±a)âˆ’1,(1+T)Ps (T)=1+Tâˆ’s.

With these conventions, the prior conclusionsUe,fabâ‰ƒZ2ef+1âٹ•C2,(kerخ؛)abâ‰ƒZ22ef+1âٹ•C4 remain consistent. New this turn: the first IA ambiguity splits into two abelian-visible square directions b[2],S[2]and genuinely nonabelian involution-indexed commutator directions Zj.

GAP: This is still not a proof of the presentation of GQ2. The missing ingredient is a dyadic formation/rigidity theorem, or an explicit arithmetic comparison, proving that the candidateâ€™s exact filtered tame action realizes the actual arithmetic Frobenius/tame-inertia action for all finite tame subextensions.

NEXT: For (e,f)=(5,4), fix a concrete tame degree-20 extension and a Frobenius-lift convention. First match the two square directions using the equivariant action on Lأ—/(Lأ—)4; then compute the five involution-indexed commutator coordinates Zj through GL (2)/GL(2)3 or GL (2)/GL(2)5.[BREAKTHROUGH]
